content:
Hi! What is the point of this Kconfig? Couldn't its uses be replaced by RUST && FW_LOADER everywhere? (The Rust part being usually redundant)
Same question for RUST_PHYLIB_ABSTRACTIONS and RUST_PWM_ABSTRACTIONS.
If you replace it, then you cannot disable it selectively, i.e. imagine you want to enable Rust for X and Y, but not you don't care about the Z abstractions and the users that use them.

But what about all other abstractions? Why currently just these? Why not example

  • RUST_I2C_ABSTRACTIONS

  • RUST_REGULATOR_ABSTRACTIONS
    or new up coming

  • RUST_SERDEV_ABSTRACTIONS

  • RUST_WMI_ABSTRACTIONS

So what is pattern for phylib, pwm and fw_loader but not for others?
I think that it'd be better if Rust abstractions could be disabled in general. But there may be a valid reason why some are treated differently.
I think we'll eventually stop doing this when number of Rust drivers grow
But for now since we have a single kernel crate, cutting unneeded abstractions can save some compilation time
